parsed_review.summary: CP00-343 is a clean descriptor-only foundation pack for RP11 (Time Expense Disbursement) that closes the RP11.P01 model phase (M08 tail, M09, M10) and opens the RP11.P02 service foundation (M00-M07) as 11 descriptor sections over 150 planned units (RP11.P01.M08.S06-RP11.P02.M07.S10). Independent verification: I hand-recomputed the deliverable distribution micro-by-micro from the baseline included_units (implementation 73, ui 24, contract 8, security_audit 16, claude_review 5, failure_recovery 10, test 14 = 150) and it matches the prompt baseline, registry deliverable_counts, manifest delivery_counts, and the live closeout-pack-plan entry exactly; phase split (P01:14 / P02:136) and required_section_rows counts also reconcile. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are sound: every descriptor result is frozen with writes_product_state:false, no_real_data:true, creates_database_rows:false, evaluates_runtime_permission:false, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false, acquires_runtime_lock:false, persists_idempotency_key:false, performs_rollback/retry_runtime:false, and executes_unit_test_runtime_paths:false, and validators.js actively enforces per-row tenant/permission/audit/validation/blocked-claim/lock-token/state-write/rollback/retry/integration-smoke no-leak invariants. Authority boundary holds: claude_is_final_approval:false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation:false (enforced), Claude packet read_only:true with tools limited to Read/Grep/Glob and the CP178+ invalid_review_blockers list. The pack does not overclaim: production_ready:false and runtime_ready:false at pack and manifest level; only the narrow Hermes production_ready_candidate (coverage.valid && foundation.valid) is true. Hermes is used solely for the descriptor-only H11 evidence packet (body excluded), not product runtime. The claimed CP00-342 P3 remediation is real: the source_email_dms_core_pack_id->source_intake_core_pack_id rename is complete across time-expense (registry/service/validators/contract), with residual occurrences only in intake-core/crm-core (legitimate upstream) and frozen CP342 artifacts/note text. Review-evidence hygiene is correct (no premature raw-output/receipt, invalid_attempts_preserved empty, readiness auth preflight exit 0 with valid JSON, forbidden_tools_present false). Unrelated dirty files (docx, workbook/*, ldip-integration invalid-attempt, new-session-handoff) are correctly classified as out of pack scope. I could not execute the npm validation suite (read-only, no Bash), but the validator and model.test.js logic is sound with meaningful assertions and command-evidence records all 23 gates at exit 0. No P0/P1/P2/P3 defects found.
